questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'this' keyword in a Java class?
options: A) To refer to the current object
B) To declare a new class
C) To extend another class
D) To implement an interface
questions.explanation: The 'this' keyword is used to refer to the current object in a class.

questions.question: How do you create a new object from a class in Java?
options: A) By using the 'new' keyword followed by the class name
B) By using the 'this' keyword followed by the class name
C) By using the 'extends' keyword followed by the class name
D) By using the 'implements' keyword followed by the class name
questions.explanation: In Java, a new object is created by using the 'new' keyword followed by the class name.

questions.question: What is the difference between a class and an object in OOP?
options: A) A class is a blueprint, while an object is an instance of the class
B) A class is an instance, while an object is a blueprint
C) A class is a variable, while an object is a data type
D) A class is a data type, while an object is a variable
questions.explanation: A class is a blueprint or a template for creating objects, while an object is an instance of the class.

questions.question: How do you declare a reference variable to hold an object in Java?
options: A) Using the 'new' keyword followed by the class name
B) Using the 'this' keyword followed by the class name
C) Using the 'extends' keyword followed by the class name
D) Using the 'implements' keyword followed by the class name
questions.explanation: A reference variable is declared using the 'type variableName;' syntax, followed by the 'new' keyword and the object.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'finalize' method in Java?
options: A) To override the constructor of a class
B) To override the destructor of a class
C) To override the finalize method of a class
D) To override the constructor of an interface
questions.explanation: The 'finalize' method is used to perform cleanup actions before an object is garbage collected in Java.
